articles.title: Vitest 4.0 Released: The Vite-Native Testing Framework
articles.description: The Vite-powered, Jest-compatible testing framework introduces visual regression testing, makes its ‘Browser Mode’ stable (for running tests in a browser directly), adds Playwright Traces support, and more. Still unsure? You can compare it with other test runners here.
articles.author: VoidZero and Contributors

articles.title: Next.js 16 Released
articles.description: Launched alongside this week’s Next.js Conf (you can ▶️ watch the livestream here), the popular React framework gains explicit caching components, an MCP server for AI-assisted debugging, both Turbopack and React Compiler support go stable, and more.
articles.author: Lai, Story, Markbåge, and Neutkens

articles.title: ▶  The Nordic.js 2025 Talk Videos
articles.description: Nordic.js took place earlier this month and they’ve just shared the videos of a fantastic set of talks. Some highlights include Christoph Porteneuve talking about ES2026 and ES2027, Kyle Simpson on passkeys, and Sara Vieira diving into writing an emulator in JavaScript.
articles.author: Nordic.js

articles.title: Importing vs Fetching JSON
articles.description: You’ve used import to load code, but what about loading JSON? Import attributes, now broadly supported across browsers, make it possible, but is there any point instead of using fetch? Jake investigates.
articles.author: Jake Archibald

articles.title: Rethinking Async Loops in JavaScript
articles.description: Awaiting in loops runs your async operations one-by-one instead of all at once. And using map() with await? It doesn’t even wait. Matt breaks down some patterns that actually work, and why forEach with async is easy to get wrong.
articles.author: Matt Smith

articles.title: Building CLIs with Ace: A Bookmarks App in Node and Bun
articles.description: Ace (GitHub repo) is a CLI app framework built by the AdonisJS folks that you might not have encountered before.
articles.author: Harminder Virk

articles.title: Solving NYT's Pips Puzzle with TypeScript
articles.description: I love doing the New York Times’ various puzzles, though I’m not a huge fan of their newest one: Pips. Nonetheless, it’s an interesting puzzle to consider how to solve algorithmically.
articles.author: Andrew Healey

articles.title: Ky 1.13: Tiny, Elegant Fetch-Based HTTP Client for Browsers
articles.description: Makes the Fetch API tidier to use (as shown here), and is also a good modern alternative for Axios. v1.13 adds a ‘context’ feature making it easier to create reusable API clients that can accept custom parameters.
articles.author: Sindre Sorhus

articles.title: JustGage 2.0: Draw and Animate Dashboard-Style SVG Gauges
articles.description: A big release for a project started over a decade ago, now brought into the modern age and using native SVG APIs. There's a playground that lets you play with the options and gauge styles.
articles.author: Bojan Djuricic

articles.title: Solito 5.0: A Way to Use React Native with Next.js
articles.description: Solito is a wrapper around React Navigation and Next.js that lets you share navigation code when building cross-platform apps. v5.0 supports Next.js 16 and Expo 54, and also drops React Native Web as a dependency.
articles.author: Fernando Rojo

articles.title: [.. 🔎..] MaxIntervalCover: Compute the Optimal Subset of Non-Overlapping Intervals
articles.author: Robert Eisele

articles.description: It's been a decade since we mentioned Backbone.js regularly, but it was a big deal in the pre-React era. Panphora looks at React vs Backbone in 2025 and ponders how much progress we've made in 15 years.
The Deno team shares some updates about its Deno Deploy platform.
There's just one week left to take the State of JavaScript 2025 survey.
The URL Pattern API is now Baseline Newly available.
Boa v0.21 – A significant release for a JavaScript engine built in Rust, including Temporal support.
Bun v1.3.1 – A point release following the epic Bun 1.3 release with more modest improvements across the board.
Biome v2.3 – The fast formatter/linter introduces Vue, Svelte and Astro support.
ESLint v9.38.0, Astro 5.15, pnpm 10.19, Rspack 1.6.0 Beta 1

articles.title: ▶  25 Rad CSS Features in 25 Minutes
articles.description: In this energetic talk, Adam gives us a tour of the latest CSS features, including field sizing, custom scrollbar styling, math functions, scroll-driven animations, and more. The slidedeck is here, if you prefer to see the list, with code samples, sans video.
articles.author: Adam Argyle

articles.title: OpenAI’s AI-Fueled Browser, ChatGPT Atlas, is Here
articles.description: Currently available on macOS only, with Windows and iOS/Android to follow, Atlas is a Chromium-based browser infused with OpenAI's take on what a hybrid chat/agent/web browser should look like.
articles.author: OpenAI

articles.title: Masonry: Watching a CSS Feature Evolve
articles.description: A high-level look at the evolution of the CSS Masonry feature, including how the proposals have evolved, and what we can learn from such developments of new CSS features.
articles.author: Saleh Mubashar

articles.title: Making Context-Aware Components: How CSS inherit() Could Simplify Design Systems
articles.description: The in-development CSS inherit() feature works in a similar way to the var() function. This new function will open up the ability to make our components naturally context-aware. Stuart shares some ideas on how this might be used and what it could mean for design systems more broadly.
articles.author: Stuart Robson

articles.title: The Two Button Problem
articles.description: Knowing when a button is active should be inherently clear, but certain design trends means that’s not always the case. Chris attempts to clear up the ambiguity that such UI implementations present.
articles.author: Chris Coyier

articles.title: The Big Gotcha with @starting-style
articles.description: Josh takes a look at this new tool (support is at around 85% right now) that lets us use CSS transitions for enter animations. He explores its intended use cases, but concludes that its utility isn’t an immediately obvious one.
articles.author: Josh W. Comeau

articles.title: Conclusions After Using Signed Exchanges on My Site for 2 Years
articles.description: Signed exchanges (SXG) is a Chrome-only Web delivery mechanism that enables authenticating the origin of a resource independently of how it was delivered, enabling privacy-preserving cross-origin prefetch. It's used by Google for serving up pages quicker to search users. This is a good summary of a technology that, sadly, seems unlikely to survive long term.
articles.author: Paweł Pokrywka

articles.title: What State ARIA In?
articles.description: An explainer looking at ‘state’ or ‘selection’ attributes like aria-selected and aria-current, clarifying what these do, when to use them, and common mistakes to avoid.
articles.author: Aditya Jainapur

articles.title: Anchor Positioning Updates for Fall 2025
articles.description: An overview of the current state of anchor positioning.
articles.author: James Stuckey Weber

articles.title: Are Your Retail Landing Pages Killing Conversions?
articles.description: Things to keep in mind as we enter the busiest online shopping period of the year.
articles.author: Tammy Everts

articles.title: Obra Icons: A Simple, Consistent Set of Icons for User Interfaces
articles.description: Currently houses more than 1000 icons that you can download as SVG, PNG, or copy the code for use in React or Svelte. Test and customize color, weight, and size of the icons live on the page.
articles.author: Obra Studio

articles.title: Web Codegen Scorer: A Tool for Evaluating the Quality of LLM-Generated Web Code
articles.description: If you're using code generation tools, this tool, from Google's Angular team, may be useful for comparing outputs by different models or monitoring quality over time.
articles.author: angular

articles.title: JustGage 2.0: Draw and Animate Dashboard-Style SVG Gauges
articles.description: A big release for a project started over a decade ago, now brought into the modern age and using native SVG APIs. The playground lets you play with the options and gauge styles.
articles.author: Bojan Djuricic

articles.title: Format JSON Online: A Complete Toolkit for JSON & Data Processing
articles.description: Includes tools to do just about anything with JSON data along with some unique ones like AI-powered utilities for fixing and generating JSON and others for sharing and collaborating.
articles.author: Format JSON Online

articles.title: Grayscale-Image-to-STL: Convert Any Grayscale Image into a 3D STL Model
articles.description: A browser-based tool using Three.JS under the hood. You can try it directly here. Niche perhaps, but a good demo and useful for reliefs, height maps, 3D printing, etc.
articles.author: sysmaya

articles.title: ColorMate: Free Color Tools for Designers & Developers
articles.description: Includes tools for generating color palettes, CSS gradients, and extracting colors from images.
articles.author: ready2up
